topic: Change-detected re-review of WIN-062 Tesla 1.574c after EXP-303 (ISS-1247/1248/1260/1261) and decider patches (ISS-1232) closed all c5 minor holes plus c4 carry-forwards. detail_evidence grew 5130->5670 (+10.5%), detail_verdict_text grew 1201->1774 (+47.7%). Verdict unchanged Misleading. Fresh c6 cycle on the patched text.
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew 1201->1774 (+47.7%); detail_evidence_length grew 5130->5670 (+10.5%); claim/finding/TLDRs/verdict unchanged. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-16T01:10:33Z (69h old, within 168h fallback threshold); obe_skipped=7 (WIN-052, WIN-054, WIN-055, WIN-033, WIN-057, WIN-063, WIN-006 all already re-reviewed since the audit ran). Underlying patches: (a) commit d4dc3a54 2026-04-20 ISS-1232 appended the Part 3.1b condition-1 failure sentence to detail_verdict_text (resolves c5 H1 carve-out undersell); (b) suggested-patches-2026-05-10T17-21 ISS-1247 replaced 'in the scientific record for over a century' with 'over a century old (Tesla 1899) and has been publicly accessible since the 1978 posthumous publication at the latest' (resolves c5 H2 sourcing-window phrasing); (c) suggested-patches-2026-05-10T17-21 ISS-1248 added 'in any independent timestamping venue (see Timestamp Error tab...)' qualifier and cross-link (resolves c5 H3 OTS pre-registration phrase); (d) suggested-patches-2026-05-11T05-28 ISS-1260 PATCH-303-D added the 1.5719c vs 1.574c paragraph to detail_evidence (resolves c5 H7 carry-forward); (e) same patch ISS-1261/ISS-1223-WIN062 PATCH-303-B replaced 'at least three' with 'three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped in commit 6e06efb' (resolves c5 H4 carry-forward and aligns with WIN-058b). c5 H5 onclick=...return false also already cleaned to onclick=showTab(timestamp) in current text. All c5 minor holes resolved.
our_argument_summary: WIN-062 (verdict: Misleading) argues, in paragraph 1 of detail_verdict_text (scientific critique): Tesla US Patent 787412 depicts a spherical Earth and describes surface wave propagation; ~1.57c is a published ELF/VLF waveguide phase velocity in the spherical Earth-ionosphere system (Galejs 1966, DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171); the dome disc_diameter (40,030 km) was fitted to globe circumference (40,075 km) by repository scripts test_curve_stretching.py and find_curve.py, so numerical agreement is construction-by-fitting, not confirmation. Paragraph 2 (post-hoc test): the Tesla velocity measurement (~1.5c; 0.08484s round-trip) was recorded in 1899 Colorado Springs experiments and published posthumously in 1978 (Nikola Tesla Museum, Beograd); the 1905 US Patent 787412 describes the same method and depicts a sphere; the measurement is over a century old and publicly accessible since 1978 at the latest; the dome adopts it, re-expresses as 1.574c using the disc diameter, and labels CONFIRMED without pre-registration in any independent timestamping venue (cross-linked to Timestamp Error tab). The verdict text now explicitly addresses BOTH Part 3.1b conditions (1) and (2) of the legitimate-novelty carve-out: condition (2) fails because a century-old published velocity does not qualify as 'not plausibly known to the author at write-time'; condition (1) fails independently because the dome re-expresses Tesla measured value using disc diameter rather than globe circumference (construction-by-fitting, not unique-to-dome derivation). The detail_evidence EXP-303 paragraph also makes explicit that Tesla own ratio (471,240/299,792=1.5719c) differs from the dome 1.574c by ~0.13%, with the gap algebraically explained as the disc_diameter (40,030 km) re-expression of Tesla round-trip distance.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No dome monitor.py domain for the 1.574c velocity claim. The dome adopts Tesla 1899 Colorado Springs Notes (per raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 207) and re-expresses the value using the fitted disc_diameter (40,030 km = 2 * 20,015 km). No external API fetch, no live comparison, no derivation from dome geometric first principles.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A - prose patches only; no monitoring code pipeline. Monitoring tag remains none.
data_sources_used: Tesla 1899 Colorado Springs Notes (published posthumously by Nikola Tesla Museum, Beograd 1978) - dome source per raw-text/08-live-power.txt Domain 2
US Patent 787412 (filed 1900, issued 1905) - spherical-Earth drawings used in our scientific critique; dome source for WIN-001 (different WIN)
Dome repository scripts test_curve_stretching.py and find_curve.py - fit disc_diameter to globe-derived WGS84 distances
Galejs 1966 DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171 - Earth-ionosphere waveguide phase-velocity kernel of truth
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: After c5 holes resolved, remaining defender openings are narrowed: (1) c5 H1 carve-out condition-1 leg now explicitly closed; (2) c5 H2 sourcing window now hedged with at-the-latest; (3) c5 H3 OTS pre-registration now cross-linked to Timestamp Error tab. The remaining minor opening is the conjunctive logic of the carve-out: addressing TWO of three conditions when only one needs to fail is rhetorically redundant; a defender could ask 'why address two?'. Strengthening artifact, not a weakness; flag as minor for completeness only.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: No new code claims introduced by the patches. The pre-existing code_analysis tags (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false) remain correct; c3/c4/c5 all confirmed these tags.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for Tesla wave speed in the dome repo. The raw-text/08-live-power.txt live dashboard shows Tesla Longitudinal Frequency (Domain 2) with hardcoded 'Source: Tesla Colorado Springs Notes 1899'; no external API fetch, no live comparison for the 1.574c velocity. Tag unchanged across c3/c4/c5/c6.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: ~1.57c is the published phase velocity for ELF/VLF waves in the spherical Earth-ionosphere waveguide (Galejs 1966, DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171). The dome keeps the value and relabels the mechanism spherical waveguide -> flat-disc aether. No new numerical prediction. Tag unchanged.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Tesla 0.08484s round-trip and ~1.5c velocity recorded in 1899 Colorado Springs experiments. Notes circulated in Tesla biographies for decades and formally published by Nikola Tesla Museum (Beograd) in 1978. Public availability predates the dome adoption by over a century (measurement) or ~48 years (publication). The dome 1.574c prediction post-dates both. No independent pre-registration of the dome value in any timestamping venue (the dome OTS anchors status_history.json, not predictions - see Timestamp Error tab).
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The disc_diameter (40,030 km) was fitted to globe circumference (40,075 km, WGS84-derived) by repository scripts test_curve_stretching.py and find_curve.py (per c2 citation check, unchanged). 1.574c is produced by combining that fitted disc_diameter with Tesla 0.08484s round-trip - globe-circumference -> fitted disc_diameter -> Tesla time -> dome prediction. The dome ratio (1.5738c rounded to 1.574c) differs from Tesla own ratio (1.5719c) by ~0.13%, with the gap explained by the disc_diameter re-expression. No dome-geometry-first derivation.

holes_found.description: Carve-out conjunctive-logic micro-redundancy. The patched paragraph now addresses BOTH Part 3.1b conditions (1) and (2) of the legitimate-novelty carve-out. Because the carve-out is conjunctive (requires all three of (1), (2), (3) to invoke it), failing ANY single condition defeats it. Addressing two is rhetorically redundant - a pedantic defender could note: 'You closed one extra leg of a carve-out that only needs one leg open to defeat. Why are you so worried about condition (1)?'. The substantive answer is that addressing both makes the case overdetermined and pre-empts the c5 H1 undersell criticism. Strengthening artifact, not a weakness; flagging as minor for completeness only. No action required.
holes_found.recommendation: No action. The redundancy is a feature, not a bug; closing two legs makes a stronger rhetorical case than closing one. If a future minor_edit pass touches this paragraph, the only optional tightening would be to make the conjunctive logic explicit (e.g. 'the carve-out, being conjunctive, requires all three conditions, so closing one suffices') but that bloats the sentence.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN consistency gap (carry-forward, partial resolution since c5 H6). EXP-303 PATCH-303-B/C aligned WIN-058b and WIN-062 to 'three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)' and 'three acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)' respectively. WIN-058 detail_evidence still says 'at least three instances in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)' and 'at least three parallel documentary concessions the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17' (two places). The 'at least three' hedge in WIN-058 is now inconsistent with WIN-058b and WIN-062 which have dropped the 'at least'. This is a WIN-058 issue not a WIN-062 issue; WIN-062 is now clean on this point. Flagging here because the c5 H6 carry was 'cross-WIN consistency' and only two of three were patched. NOT a hole in WIN-062; reporting for cross-WIN context.
holes_found.recommendation: Open a separate WIN-058 patch (analyst-baby BAU candidate): replace 'at least three' with 'three' in WIN-058 detail_evidence (two occurrences). This synchronizes the three WINs and closes the c5 H6 carry across the WIN-058 / WIN-058b / WIN-062 triplet. Low priority - the cross-WIN reader who notices the discrepancy will see WIN-058 saying 'at least three' while WIN-058b says 'three' for the same set of concessions.

holes_found.description: Sourcing-window phrasing - residual opening. 'The measurement is over a century old (Tesla 1899) and has been publicly accessible since the 1978 posthumous publication at the latest' is a substantial tightening of the c5 'in the scientific record for over a century' phrase. The 'at the latest' hedge is correct and pre-empts the obvious pedantry. However, a defender could still note that pre-1978 Tesla biographies (notably John J. O-Neill, Prodigal Genius, 1944) discussed the Colorado Springs experiments, so the velocity figure was effectively in circulation long before 1978. The 'at the latest' phrasing implicitly admits earlier accessibility, so the opening is largely closed - but the text does not name any pre-1978 secondary source that would make the point explicit. This is a stretching-the-pedantry opening, not a substantive challenge.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: name a pre-1978 secondary source if one is available (e.g. 'circulated in Tesla biographies from at least 1944 (J.J. O-Neill, Prodigal Genius) and formally published in 1978'). Not required; the 'at the latest' hedge is sufficient. Carry forward to a future minor_edit pass.

stronger_arguments.description: The disc_diameter-as-fitted-parameter argument (carry from c4/c5, still strongest). The detail_evidence already makes the construction-by-fitting case explicitly (test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py), and the EXP-303 ISS-1260 paragraph now shows the arithmetic of how 40,030 km * Tesla time produces 1.574c rather than Tesla own 1.5719c. The verdict text paragraph 2 now also names this in the condition-1 leg (using disc diameter rather than globe circumference, producing numerical agreement by construction). The argument is now well-distributed across detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. No promotion needed; the case is made.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-062 detail_evidence EXP-303 paragraph (471,240/299,792=1.5719c and 40,030/0.08484/c=1.5738c)
data/wins.json WIN-062 detail_verdict_text paragraph 2 final sentence (condition 1 also fails independently)

stronger_arguments.description: Advocate-mode for the defender reading the c6-state paragraph. The most resourceful defender would now say: 'OK, granting the post-hoc test - your strongest move is 1.574c is just globe physics relabeled. But the dome would reply: of course we use disc geometry, that is the whole point of the model. You cannot simultaneously argue (a) the dome is wrong because it is a different geometry and (b) the dome is wrong because re-expressing Tesla value in dome geometry is not an independent derivation - one of these has to give.' Our reply: the dome makes a positive claim that its 1.574c is a CONFIRMATION of the disc geometry (per the wins.html badge). For that claim to bite, the dome would need to produce a value Tesla could not have known, derived from dome geometry without backreference to globe distances. Instead the dome fits disc_diameter to the globe circumference (per its own scripts) and then claims the rederived value as confirmation - which is the textbook circularity that Refuted-by-Data or Misleading verdicts target. The current detail_evidence frames this correctly. No new defender opening surfaces in c6.
sources: data/wins.json WIN-062 detail_evidence (existing construction-by-fitting argument plus EXP-303 arithmetic)
monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-062.c5.json stronger_arguments[1] (advocate-mode analysis from c5; same defender move, now pre-empted by patches)

kernel_of_truth.description: The c6 state of WIN-062 is a clean example of cumulative patching working as designed. The c5 review identified three minor rhetorical openings (carve-out undersell, sourcing window pedantry, OTS canary cross-reference) and two carry-forwards (1.5719c vs 1.574c arithmetic; cross-WIN phrasing alignment). All five were resolved by patches between 2026-04-20 (ISS-1232 in d4dc3a54) and 2026-05-11 (EXP-303). The patches were drawn directly from the c5 recommendations (data/wins.json:062 c5 holes_found recommendations match the find/replace strings in the patch files almost verbatim for ISS-1247, ISS-1248, ISS-1260, ISS-1261). The Misleading verdict is now textually overdetermined: scientific critique stands on Tesla own patent drawings plus the Galejs 1966 waveguide kernel of truth; the post-hoc test stands on both Part 3.1b conditions (1) and (2); the arithmetic of construction-by-fitting is explicit in detail_evidence (data/wins.json:062.detail_evidence EXP-303 paragraph).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The c6 state strengthens but does not change the verdict. The dome 1.574c figure remains an algebraic re-expression of (globe-circumference / Tesla-time) using a fitted disc_diameter that was itself derived from WGS84 distances per the dome own scripts (data/wins.json:062.detail_evidence test_curve_stretching.py / find_curve.py reference). The kernel-of-truth analysis remains: the ~1.57c phase velocity in the Earth-ionosphere waveguide is genuine physics (Galejs 1966, DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171); the dome relabels this as evidence for a flat disc. The post-hoc test failure is now overdetermined (both Part 3.1b conditions (1) and (2) explicitly fail per data/sections.json:part3 posthoc-standard text). The remaining minor openings (carve-out conjunctive-logic redundancy, sourcing window pre-1978 secondary sources, cross-WIN consistency with WIN-058) do not touch the Misleading verdict - they are rhetorical strengthening opportunities, not substantive challenges. Recommended action is no_change for the WIN-062 text itself, with a separate WIN-058 patch to align cross-WIN phrasing.

deeper_analysis:
The c5 -> c6 transition is the cleanest patch-cycle convergence in WIN-062 history. The c4 -> c5 transition fixed the Tesla source attribution (1905 patent -> 1899 Notes); c5 -> c6 closes all five remaining minor openings (three c5-fresh, two c4-carry-forwards). The recommended_action shift to no_change reflects the converged state: no further patches needed for WIN-062 specifically. Confidence rises 0.85 (c4) -> 0.9 (c5) -> 0.93 (c6). The 0.07 gap from 1.0 reflects (i) the carve-out conjunctive-logic redundancy (cosmetic), (ii) the residual pre-1978 secondary-source opening (pedantry-level), (iii) the cross-WIN consistency gap with WIN-058 (not a WIN-062 issue but a cross-WIN observable).

Quantitative verification: I re-ran the four key arithmetic claims. (1) Tesla own ratio 471,240 / 299,792.458 = 1.571887c, text says 1.5719c, agrees to 4 sig figs. (2) Globe interpretation 40,075 km / 0.08484 s = 472,360 km/s = 1.575622c, text says 472,360, agrees exactly. (3) Dome disc-diameter 40,030 km / 0.08484 s = 471,829 km/s = 1.573853c, text says 1.5738c rounded to 1.574c, agrees exactly. (4) Round-trip dome derivation 40,030 km * 2 / 0.08484 s = 943,659 km/s = 3.1477c, text says 3.148c round-trip, agrees to rounding. All quantitative claims in the patched text verify.

Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz, sourced to US Patent 787412 1905) and WIN-062 (Tesla 1.574c, sourced to 1899 Colorado Springs Notes) have disjoint Tesla-document attribution as established by the c4 -> c5 patch. WIN-058 / WIN-058b / WIN-062 EXP-181 acknowledge-without-fix paragraphs are now mostly aligned via EXP-303 PATCH-303-B/C, with WIN-058 still carrying the 'at least three' hedge in two places (flagged as hole 2 above). WIN-001 verdict Refuted by Data vs WIN-062 verdict Misleading reflect different fault modes (formula error vs measurement re-expression) and are mutually consistent, not contradictory.

Advocate-mode lens: the strongest dome defender move on the c6 state would be (i) challenge the conjunctive-logic redundancy (cosmetic - hole 1), (ii) challenge 'publicly accessible since 1978' on pre-1978 biographies (pedantry - hole 3), or (iii) try to re-open the 'derivation is unique to the dome framework' leg by arguing disc_diameter is a primary dome parameter. Move (iii) is the only substantive one; it is preempted by detail_evidence citing the dome own fitting scripts (test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py).

Holistic fit: the patched WIN-062 is publishable as-is. The case for the Misleading verdict is now overdetermined - scientific critique paragraph 1 closes via patent drawings plus waveguide physics; post-hoc test paragraph 2 closes via both Part 3.1b conditions (1) and (2); arithmetic verification stands in detail_evidence. No verdict_change or rewrite warranted; minor_edit only if cross-WIN alignment with WIN-058 is pursued.

US Patent 787412 - confirmed 1905 grant on Google Patents; cited correctly for wave-propagation method and spherical-Earth drawings (NOT for velocity value itself)
DOI 10.1029/JZ071i004p01171 (Galejs 1966) - Earth-ionosphere waveguide phase velocity kernel of truth, unchanged across cycles

Part 3.1b carve-out three-condition text - verified verbatim in sections.json part3 (condition 1 = unique-to-dome derivation; condition 2 = not plausibly known at write-time; condition 3 = published-together)
Nikola Tesla Museum (Beograd) 1978 posthumous publication of Colorado Springs Notes - consistent with dome attribution; standard secondary source citation
Quantitative claims (1.5719c, 1.5738c, 472,360 km/s, 3.148c) all verified by direct computation - all values match to rounding

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Cross-WIN phrasing variant on the 2026-04-17 acknowledge-without-fix concessions: WIN-058 still has 'at least three' in two places while WIN-058b and WIN-062 are aligned on 'three parallel / three acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped in commit 6e06efb'. WIN-062 is now clean on this point. WIN-058 needs the matching patch to fully resolve the c5 H6 carry across the triplet.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Granting your post-hoc test, your central anti-WIN-062 move is that the 1.574c is just (globe-circumference / Tesla-time) relabeled. But you cannot consistently argue both that (a) the dome geometry is wrong because it differs from the globe, and (b) re-expressing Tesla value in dome geometry is not an independent derivation because dome ~ globe by construction. If dome geometry is meaningfully different from globe geometry, then expressing 1.574c via disc_diameter IS a distinct derivation. If dome geometry is just globe geometry relabeled, then your scientific critique loses bite - there is no flat-disc claim to attack.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Already preempted in detail_evidence: the dome own scripts (test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py) explicitly fit disc_diameter to WGS84 distances. The dome geometry is admitted-by-its-own-code to be a relabeling fit, not a primary parameter. The defense survives only as long as the dome does not admit this fitting in code; once the fitting is shown, the defense collapses. No further preemption needed in detail_verdict_text - the case is made in detail_evidence.

claims_checked: Globe interpretation 40,075/0.08484 = 472,360 km/s = 1.5756c (text claim 472,360 km/s, 0.24% diff from Tesla)
Dome disc-diameter re-expression 40,030/0.08484 = 471,829 km/s = 1.5738c rounds to 1.574c (text claim 1.5738c rounded to 1.574c)
Round-trip dome arithmetic 40,030*2/0.08484 = 943,659 km/s = 3.148c (text claim 3.148c round-trip = 1.574c one-way)

summary_for_decider: WIN-062 c5 -> c6: all five c5 holes resolved (three c5-fresh: H1 carve-out undersell, H2 sourcing window, H3 OTS canary cross-ref; two carry-forwards: H4 'at least three', H7 1.5719c vs 1.574c arithmetic). c6 verdict_holds=true, confidence 0.93, recommended_action=no_change. Three minor holes flagged for completeness, none requiring WIN-062 action: H1 carve-out conjunctive-logic redundancy (cosmetic, intentional), H2 cross-WIN consistency with WIN-058 (a WIN-058 issue, not WIN-062 - candidate for analyst-baby BAU patch on WIN-058 detail_evidence to replace 'at least three' with 'three' in two places), H3 pre-1978 secondary-source pedantry (already hedged with 'at the latest'). All quantitative claims verified by direct computation. Cross-WIN consistency with WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz) and WIN-058b is clean. No action needed on WIN-062. Consider opening a WIN-058 patch as the only follow-on item.
